summary: SignWell e-signature integration — International Dialing Waiver

new: SignWell e-signature integration — International Dialing Waiver sent automatically from Voice quote Documents panel with one click
Admin → Integrations → SignWell: API key and International Dialing Waiver Template ID are now configurable in the UI — no code changes needed when templates are replaced
Test Connection button on SignWell integration card verifies API key before saving
Waiver document uses SignWell text tags ({{signature}}, {{text}}, {{date}}) for automatic field placement — no manual field setup required
Signature block includes Full Legal Name, Company Name, Title/Role (blank fields for client to complete) and auto-locked signing date from audit trail
Waiver drawer includes Proposal/Deal Name (auto-populated from quote), Reference # (quote number), and Authorized Scope / Account field — specifies which location or account the authorization covers
SignWell webhook handler auto-updates quote status when client signs
Send Reminder and Check Status buttons on waiver panel for tracking pending signatures
Preview Document toggle shows full waiver content before sending
improved: Signature block fields sized to 550×26px (text) and 550×80px (signature) for legible signed output
Date field replaced with static document date — actual signing timestamp captured in SignWell audit trail (legally more defensible than client-entered date)
DOCUMENT REFERENCE table in waiver header ties authorization to a specific proposal, quote number, and account scope — critical for clients with multiple locations
Proxy now surfaces full SignWell error JSON instead of generic HTTP status code — much easier to debug
fixed: SignWell was rejecting requests due to wrong field names (signees vs recipients, fields vs signing_elements, [[]] vs {{}} text tag format)
Documents going to drafts instead of sending — signing_elements:[] was overriding text tags; removed, replaced with with_signature_page then text_tags approach
draft:false added explicitly to ensure documents send immediately

summary: Hosted Voice — Assumptions & Exclusions document + out-of-scope rate schedule

new: Voice Assumptions & Exclusions modal — voice-specific document separate from Managed IT assumptions. Covers: customer story/discovery notes, current provider, why switching, seats/DIDs/locations, number porting (none/partial/full with DID list), feature flags (auto-attendant, voicemail, call recording), fax lines, internet notes, hardware notes, current PBX type, network/firewall notes, compliance requirements (HIPAA/SOC2, PCI/CMMC)

10 pre-populated standard voice exclusions: end-user training, cabling, internet connectivity, third-party integrations, number porting limits, international calling, on-site support, off-scope config changes, hardware procurement, fax beyond agreed scope
Out-of-scope rate schedule added to Voice Documents panel — same market-adjusted T&M rate card as IT/Bundle/FlexIT quotes. Applies to all labor outside contracted voice scope
Voice Documents panel now shows three cards in order: Out-of-Scope Rate Schedule, Assumptions & Exclusions, International Dialing Waiver
Bundle strategy: IT assumptions and Voice assumptions are permanently separate documents — each quote type owns its own. Bundle will stack both independently
improved: Voice quote fetches market_rate_analyses on ZIP entry to power the rate sheet — synthetic fallback built from selectedMkt if no DB record exists

summary: HubSpot Voice fixes — contact population and crash on connect

fixed: marketTiers was not destructured from useConfig() in VoiceQuotePage — caused crash when HubSpot connected a deal with a ZIP code on file
selectedMkt state was missing entirely from VoiceQuotePage — setSelectedMkt() call in onConnect handler crashed mid-execution, preventing contact name and email from populating
Contact Name and Contact Email now populate correctly from HubSpot on Voice quotes — these were blank because the onConnect handler was crashing before reaching those lines
All 15 state setters in Voice onConnect handler audited and verified — no further undeclared state variables

summary: Market rate locking — saved quotes never auto-refresh, 3-month stale threshold

new: Market rates now LOCK on saved quotes — opening a saved IT, Voice, or Bundle quote reads the existing DB analysis only. No AI call, no rate change. Rates stay at what was pulled when the quote was created
readOnly mode added to getOrAnalyzeMarket() — fifth parameter, passes through the entire call chain. Guaranteed no AI will fire in readOnly mode regardless of staleness
fallbackMarket prop on MarketRateCard — if no DB record exists for the quote's ZIP, the card builds a synthetic display from the locked market tier on the quote. Card never disappears
improved: STALE_MONTHS changed from 6 → 3 months — market analyses are considered stale after 3 months. Market Intelligence tab shows updated badge: '3+ months — refresh needed'
MarketRateCard shows 'Rate locked' instead of epoch date (12/31/1969) when analyzed_at is null on fallback object
Stale badge suppressed when analyzed_at is null — fallback display objects are not incorrectly flagged as stale
MultiSiteQuotePage uses readOnly=true when loading saved quotes — per-location market analyses read DB only

getMarketByZip() now searches both the zip column AND the zip_codes array — previously only searched primary ZIP, causing misses for markets with multiple associated ZIPs (root cause of blank rates and epoch date on FIT-2026-1000)
fixed: MarketRateCard was re-fetching and potentially calling AI every time a saved quote was opened — clientZip being restored from saved data triggered the useEffect unconditionally

FIT-2026-1000 showing 12/31/1969 stale + blank rates — ZIP 61081 is a secondary ZIP in zip_codes array, not the primary zip column. getMarketByZip was returning null causing fallback synthetic object with no rates to display
Market Intelligence admin controls confirmed intact: per-market Refresh and Delete buttons, Analyze new market, stale badge at correct 3-month threshold

summary: Voice quote — hardware mix & match, BYOH, international dialing waiver, flex time removed

new: Hardware mix & match — select multiple Yealink models with quantities on a single quote (e.g., 10 T33G + 5 T46U + 2 W60B). Added Yealink T57W and W60B models

BYOH (Bring Your Own Handset) — new hardware option for client-owned 3CX-compatible devices. Enter model and quantity; wipe & register fee applied per device at configurable rate (Admin → Pricing Settings → voice_byoh_fee, default $20)
Evergreen Lease vs Outright Purchase are now clearly mutually exclusive — radio button selection prevents mixing
📦 Shipping notice on purchase orders — 'billed at end of implementation at then-current UPS rates' displayed as a prominent note
⚠ International Dialing Authorization & Liability Waiver — new document. Opens from Voice Documents panel when international dialing is enabled, or can be created manually at any time
Waiver captures: client entity, authorized contact, title, dialing tier selection. Three tiers: Standard International, Extended International, Full Open Access
Full waiver text: toll fraud liability, no cap on charges, client assumes 100% financial responsibility, security responsibility, right to suspend, indemnification clause
Waiver exports directly to Smart Pricing Table via API for client signature or can be saved to the quote
improved: Voice Documents panel added to right column — shows waiver status (red Required badge when international dialing is active)
fixed: FlexTimeMeter removed from Hosted Voice quote — voice quotes have no included flex time; onsite support requires a Managed IT contract

summary: FlexTimeMeter on all IT quote types + two-tone progress bar for included vs add-on time

new: FlexTimeMeter component now appears on all managed IT quote types: IT, Hosted Voice, Bundle (IT+Voice), and Multi-Location
Two-tone progress bar: blue segment = included time from package, orange segment = purchased add-on block time — instantly visible distinction
Legend below bar shows exact hours for each segment with color-coded labels
When an add-on block is selected on the IT quote, the meter updates live to show combined total hours per month
improved: Multi-Location meter totals workstations and users across all locations for an accurate flex time pool display
Meter shows 'By Request' state when package has no included time and no block purchased, with a prompt to purchase a block
fixed: FlexTimeMeter extracted from inline IIFE in QuotePage into a shared reusable component — now consistent across all four IT quote types

summary: Flex Time Blocks — managed IT monthly add-on and FlexIT pre-purchase annual blocks

new: ⏱ Flex Time Blocks added to all managed IT quotes — five standard blocks: 5hr (Starter), 10hr (Standard), 20hr (Extended), 30hr (Pro), 40hr (Enterprise)
Market-adjusted pricing: each block is priced at a discount off the market T&M remote support rate (10–25% depending on block size)
⚡ FlexIT On-Demand block purchasing — same blocks available as one-time pre-purchases on FlexIT quotes. Valid 12 months, refillable at the original agreed rate
Rate lock on FlexIT blocks: if client refills within 30 days of depletion or expiration, original rate is honored. After 30 days a new quote at current rates is required

Tier 3 exclusion clearly stated: network design/engineering, server projects, firewall/SAN deployment, email migration, DR, software engineering, and security audits are bid separately at the Tier 3 rate and cannot be pooled in blocks
Admin → Packages: new Flex Time Model selector per package — None / Included (X min/WS/mo) / Required / All-Inclusive
improved: FlexIT billing summary updates when a flex block is selected — shows block purchase as a separate line with 12-month validity note
Flex Time block detail card shows: effective rate per hour, T&M comparison rate, savings amount, and full terms with Tier 3 exclusion

summary: FlexIT Payment Schedule & Terms — full legal document matching SPT template

new: 💳 Payment Schedule & Terms document added to FlexIT documents panel — opens as a right-side drawer matching the SPT template exactly
Payment schedule shows: Month to Month term, Payment #1 (prepayment at signing, non-refundable), out-of-scope language

Full legal billing sections included: Automatic Payments (Required) with billing portal link, Invoicing Terms (Due Upon Receipt), Paper Check Payments ($10 fee), Purchase Orders, Setup Services Fee, Declined/Returned Payments ($50 fee), Hardware/Software/Licensing all sales final
Area 2 surcharge notice automatically shown when client location is in a metro surcharge market
improved: FlexIT documents panel now shows two cards: Rate Card and Payment Schedule — each with View/Export action

summary: FlexIT documents and billing corrections

improved: FlexIT documents panel now shows Rate Sheet only — Assumptions and Payment Schedule cards removed (assumptions are the fixed 7-bullet T&M language shown inline; payment schedule replaced by FlexIT-specific billing summary)
FlexIT billing summary on the quote page corrected to reflect T&M model: Payment #1 prepayment at signing + Ongoing Labor billed as consumed, invoiced on completion. No monthly recurring fee language
fixed: FlexIT was showing managed IT payment schedule (NET 20, monthly billing commencement) — replaced with correct Due Upon Receipt, month-to-month T&M structure

summary: FlexIT MSA language removed — break/fix description corrected

fixed: Removed 'No MSA Required' from FlexIT quote page header and nav dropdown — all Ferrum agreements require a signed MSA. Description updated to 'Time & Materials — Break/Fix' in both locations

summary: FlexIT added to top nav New Quote dropdown

improved: ⚡ FlexIT On-Demand now appears in the top navigation bar New Quote dropdown alongside all other quote types
fixed: FlexIT was only appearing in the Saved Quotes page dropdown — missing from the always-visible nav bar dropdown in Layout.js

summary: QuotesPage crash fix — showNewMenu state missing

fixed: Saved Quotes page crashed on load — showNewMenu state variable referenced in JSX but never declared. Fixed with useState(false) declaration

summary: FlexIT save payload fix — removed non-existent DB columns

fixed: FlexIT quotes failed to save — payload included quote_type, contract_term_months, monthly_fee, onboarding_fee, contract_value columns that don't exist in the quotes table
Removed next_quote_sequence RPC call that doesn't exist — quote numbers are auto-generated by the DB trigger like all other quote types
Financial data now stored in totals JSONB field consistent with other quote types

summary: VoiceQuotePage crash fix — sptProposalId state missing

fixed: Hosted Voice quote page crashed on load — sptProposalId and setSptProposalId referenced in SPTConnect component but state was never declared. Added useState(null) declaration and DB restore on quote load

summary: FlexIT On-Demand quote type — time & materials with market-adjusted rate card

new: ⚡ FlexIT On-Demand — new quote type for time & materials / break-fix engagements with no MSA required
New Quote button upgraded to a dropdown menu listing all five quote types: Managed IT, Hosted Voice, Bundle, Multi-Location IT, FlexIT On-Demand
FlexIT quotes auto-generate a market-adjusted Rate Card from the market intelligence data for the client's ZIP code
Prepayment calculator: enter hours (default 2) × market remote support rate = prepayment amount. Override available if needed
Area 2 surcharge (+30%) automatically applied and shown when client ZIP is in a metro surcharge market
Full document suite: Rate Sheet export, Assumptions (7 fixed T&M bullets from the SPT template), Payment Schedule (Month to Month, Due Upon Receipt)
HubSpot deal connect and SPT proposal link/create — same as all other quote types
FlexIT quotes appear in Saved Quotes list with an orange FlexIT badge
Quote number format: FIT-FLEX-####

summary: MultiSite page crash fix — inputs undefined in DocumentsPanel

fixed: MultiSite page crashed on load — DocumentsPanel was referencing an inputs object that doesn't exist on MultiSite (which uses individual state vars). Fixed by constructing the inputs object from actual MultiSite state variables (locations, contractTerm, compliance)

summary: SPTConnect JSX build fix — malformed closing tags on all four quote pages

fixed: Build failure caused by SPTConnect insertion landing inside HubSpotConnect props — HubSpotConnect closing /> was missing and SPTConnect had a stray />. Fixed on all four quote pages (IT, Voice, Bundle, MultiSite)

summary: SPTConnect — link or create SPT proposals from all quote pages

new: SPTConnect widget added to all four quote types (IT, Voice, Bundle, Multi-Site) — sits directly below HubSpotConnect in the left column
🔍 Link Existing mode — searches your Smart Pricing Table account, shows proposal name, status (DRAFT/SENT/WON/LOST), last updated date and creator. Click any row to link
✚ Create New mode — pre-fills proposal name as [Client] — Managed IT Services ([Quote#]), creates empty proposal in SPT via API and links it instantly
Open in SPT → link shows on connected quotes. Unlink (✕) removes the association without deleting the SPT proposal
If SPT API key is not configured, both actions show a clear message directing to Admin → Integrations
improved: SPT proposal name fetched from API on quote load — shows actual proposal name instead of truncated ID

summary: Documents panel on Bundle and Multi-Site + AI assumptions generation + MultiSite crash fix

new: Documents panel (Rate Sheet, Assumptions, Payment Schedule) added to Bundle and Multi-Site quote pages — consistent across all IT quote types

✨ Generate Assumptions with AI — button below the Customer Story field. Rep types raw discovery notes, AI generates structured professional assumptions: environment overview, user assumptions, infrastructure notes, cloud/technology, key requirements, compliance notes
AI uses claude-haiku for fast generation — output saved with the quote and shown as a collapsible preview
improved: Assumptions: Mobile/Field Technicians section renamed to Task Workers vs Power Users — clearer explanation of why these workers are excluded from Power User pricing
Assumptions: Laptops & Workstations field added at the top of the Infrastructure section
Assumptions: Why switching text area added under Current Provider for additional context beyond checkboxes
Bundle DocumentsPanel uses IT-side inputs, package, products, and compliance for assumptions generation
fixed: MultiSite obIncentive state was missing — page crashed on load with white screen

summary: Assumptions & Payment Schedule match actual SPT document structure

improved: Assumptions now includes: supported user list (names/roles), mobile/field technician section with pricing exclusion explanation, core infrastructure inventory (servers, network, telephony), cloud services narrative, infrastructure modernization opportunities, editable deliverables section, and all 10 standard exclusions from the actual SPT template

Payment Schedule restructured to match SPT exactly: Payment #1 (onboarding, due at signing, non-refundable), monthly billing commencement trigger (onboarding complete OR 30 days), out-of-scope language, automatic payment requirement with billing portal URL, NET 20 terms, $50 declined/returned payment fee, hardware/software all sales final language, purchase orders section
fixed: NET 30 terms corrected to NET 20 to match Ferrum billing policy

summary: SPT link existing proposals + search modal

new: 🔍 Link Existing button in Rate Sheet drawer — searches your Smart Pricing Table account and links any existing proposal to the Ferrum IQ quote
SPT search modal shows proposal name, client, status (DRAFT/SENT/WON), last updated date, and creator — click any row to link
Unlink option in the Rate Sheet drawer — removes the association (proposal stays in SPT)
Documents panel shows green SPT ✓ badge when a proposal is linked
improved: SPT export bar replaced with full connect experience: Link Existing + Create New buttons, status indicator, and Open in SPT link
fixed: Duplicate supabase import in RateSheetModal causing build failure

summary: Documents panel — Out-of-Scope Rate Sheet, Assumptions & Exclusions, Payment Schedule with Smart Pricing Table integration

new: 📄 Documents panel on every IT quote — three document cards: Out-of-Scope Rate Sheet, Assumptions & Exclusions, Payment Schedule

Out-of-Scope Rate Sheet: market-adjusted rates for all four sections (Remote Labor, Dev/App, On-Site, After Hours) pulled directly from market analysis data. Area 2 surcharge (+30%) auto-applied for NYC, SF, Seattle, Chicago metro, Hawaii, Alaska, Las Vegas, Canada
Smart Pricing Table integration — export any document to SPT via API. API key stored in Admin → Integrations → Smart Pricing Table
SPT proxy Netlify function keeps API key server-side — never exposed to browser

Assumptions & Exclusions form saves discovery notes, user list, field tech details, infrastructure inventory, M365/cloud services, modernization opportunities, deliverables, and exclusions back to the quote
Payment Schedule shows term header, Payment #1 onboarding at signing, monthly billing commencement trigger, incentive adjustments, and all billing terms
spt_proposal_id and spt_synced_at added to quotes table to track SPT linkage
improved: All fixed OOS fees (same-day, next-day, cancellation, abort, after-hours dispatch, Area 2 surcharge) stored in pricing_settings — admin-editable without a deploy

summary: Market Intelligence zip_codes array + admin delete + improved multi-zip search

new: zip_codes array on each market record — every ZIP that resolves to a city is accumulated over time (e.g., Elgin accumulates 60120, 60123, 60124)
ZIP codes shown as tag pills in both the market table row and the expanded detail panel
Admin delete button on each market row (admin only) — removes the record with confirmation. Next analysis re-creates it fresh from USPS + AI. No more SQL needed for cleanup
improved: Search now checks the full zip_codes array — searching 60120 returns Elgin even if only 60124 is the primary stored zip
saveMarketAnalysis now merges new zips into the zip_codes array on every lookup — builds the mapping automatically over time

summary: Price lock — approved quotes freeze rates to prevent unexpected pricing changes

new: Price lock triggers automatically when a quote is saved with Approved status — rates, package pricing, and settings are frozen at that moment
🔒 Prices locked banner appears at the top of any locked quote showing the lock date and package name
Unlock Pricing button visible to the assigned rep (their own quotes) or any admin user
Unlock confirmation modal warns about price discrepancy risk if quote has already been sent or exported to Smart Pricing Table
Unlocking writes an entry to quote revision history for a full audit trail
Price lock applies across all quote types: Managed IT, Hosted Voice, Bundle, Multi-Site
improved: calcQuote now accepts a pricing snapshot — uses frozen rates when locked, live rates for draft/in-review quotes

summary: Onboarding incentive fixes + ZIP city accuracy + Bundle/MultiSite onboarding

new: Onboarding Incentive added to Bundle and Multi-Site quotes — applies to combined onboarding/NRC fee
Onboarding incentive selections now write to quote revision history — rep can see when and what incentive was applied or changed
improved: Market intelligence ZIP lookup now uses USPS data before calling AI — fixes cities like Elgin (60124) being returned as Aurora
Onboarding incentive card no longer disappears after selecting an option — stays visible so rep can change selection at any time
No Incentive option keeps the card visible and marks it selected rather than dismissing
Modify button returns to the selection screen without resetting
Contract TCV KPI card now correctly reflects the applied onboarding discount
fixed: Onboarding incentive selection was not updating the Contract TCV pricing card — TCV now uses effectiveFee

summary: Bundle quote layout — Combined Deal Summary on right column, consistent with other quote types

improved: Combined Deal Summary moved to the right column — matches IT and Voice quote layout
Voice card now stacks under Managed IT card in the left column
Quote Notes and Quote History moved under Combined Deal Summary in the right column
Cost Model card in left column below Voice breakdown
fixed: Stray closing div that caused JSX fragment build error

summary: Market Intelligence search fix, packages save fix, layout and consistency updates

improved: Market Intelligence search now correctly matches city, state, and zip — null city/state values no longer break the entire search
Packages tab in Admin now shows a clear error message when save fails instead of silently doing nothing
Market Intelligence refresh now works for markets without a zip code — uses city+state for analysis
Market Intelligence refresh shows a blue spinning status indicator while AI analysis is running
fixed: Packages save was failing silently — $location and other display-only fields were being sent to Supabase which rejected them
Market Intelligence search was throwing a TypeError on null city/state fields, returning no results
Market Intelligence refresh button was broken for markets with bad/non-numeric zip codes (like MOBIL)

summary: Market Intelligence as standalone nav page + layout consistency across all quote types

new: Market Intelligence moved from Admin tab to main navigation — accessible to all team members
All users can view market data, search, filter, and analyze new markets — admin-only for editing rates and refreshing
Quote Notes now present on Voice and Multi-Site quote pages — matches IT quote behavior
Assigned Sales Rep added to Voice and Bundle Proposal Details sections — consistent across all four quote types
Proposal Details section renamed from Client Details on Bundle — uniform naming across all pages
improved: Bundle page Quote Status moved to Package section — consistent with IT and Voice pages
Bundle helper functions (Sec, Fld, TI, NI, SI, Tog, LI) updated to match IT page font sizes and spacing
Voice and Bundle Proposal Details now include rep selector with commission rate display
Contract term and status now appear at the top of the left column on Voice quotes
fixed: QuoteNotes was wrapped in existingQuote conditional on Voice and MultiSite — matched IT page pattern (unconditional with optional chaining)
Market Intelligence tab renamed to Market Intelligence throughout
ZIP codes with non-numeric values (city abbreviations from seed data) now display blank instead of the raw text
Market Intelligence zip search now normalizes both query and stored value to 5 digits

summary: HubSpot shared component across all quote types + UI consistency fixes

new: HubSpotConnect shared component — consistent connect/search/sync/disconnect across all four quote types
Deal Description / Information field in HubSpot panel — syncs to HubSpot on save
View Deal link and Sync button in HubSpot panel — available on all quote types
improved: Duplicate Deal Description fields removed from IT, Voice, and Bundle right columns — now lives exclusively in the HubSpot panel
Voice quote Deal Summary moved up to match IT quote position
Multi-Site left column width matched to IT quote (345px)
Rate abbreviations standardized to WS/US/LOC across all pages and Admin
fixed: Voice and Bundle pages were missing Sync, View link, deal description, and disconnect — all now present via shared component

summary: Multi-Site IT Quotes — fair market pricing across multiple locations with volume discount

new: Multi-Site IT quote type (FIT-MS- prefix) — New Quote → 🏢 Multi-Site IT
Location builder — add unlimited locations, each with name, address, zip, type, users, workstations, servers, endpoints
Standard vs Restricted location types — Standard includes site management fee and network layer; Restricted (co-work/shared/3rd-party) is users and devices only, no network layer, no site fee
Site management fee pulls from the selected package (pkg.location_rate) — never hardcoded
Per-location market analysis — zip code triggers AI market rate lookup, pricing multiplier applied per location
Multi-location volume discount: 2-4 locations = 3%, 5-9 = 5%, 10-19 = 8%, 20+ = 10% (stored in pricing_settings, fully configurable)
Contract term discount stacks on top of multi-location discount
Add-on products applied once at quote level — qty scales to total users/workstations across all locations
Per-location on-site and dispatch rate card shown in the expanded location breakdown
Covered Locations section on the quote — legally binding list of all addresses covered under the agreement
Convert to Single Location — opens a new standard IT quote with location 1 data, multi-site quote stays in history
SPT export includes full covered_locations_assumption field with every address listed
HubSpot connect, Quote Notes, Send for Review, version history all work on multi-site quotes
improved: New Quote dropdown now has four options: Managed IT, Hosted Voice, Bundle Quote, Multi-Site IT
Saved Quotes list shows purple Multi-Site badge and routes correctly

summary: Company logo branding + KPI strip layout fix + opSubtotal crash fix

new: Company logo on login page — shown on a dark background to match logo styling
Company logo in the top-left navigation bar replacing the placeholder F icon
Logo upload in Admin → Integrations — upload a PNG/SVG/JPG file or paste a public URL
Logo stored in pricing_settings and loaded dynamically — update without redeploying
improved: Term Comparison and Compliance Recommendation panels moved above the KPI cards — no longer inside the 4-column grid, so Contract TCV stays inline with Monthly MRR, Onboarding, and Implied GM
fixed: opSubtotal ReferenceError — variable was referenced in calcQuote return but not declared after discount logic rewrite — caused white screen on all pages

summary: Product discount/commission protection + per-rep commission rates + assigned rep on quotes

new: No Discount flag on products — MSRP products pass through at full vendor price, never discounted
No Commission flag on products — vendor pass-through products excluded from rep commission base
Assigned Sales Rep field on every Managed IT quote — defaults to the logged-in user, changeable at any time
Per-user commission rate in Admin → Users — set individually per team member (e.g. 8%, 10%, 12%)
Global commission rate fallback in Admin → Pricing Settings — applies when no user rate is set
Commission priority chain: rep rate → global rate → 0%
Commission line in cost model shows rep name, rate, and commissionable base — fully transparent
Net MRR after commission and GM after commission shown in cost model
Protected product revenue shown as separate line — clear audit trail of what's protected
Flags visible in Admin → Products table — 🔒 No Discount · 💼 No Comm badges
Compliance-based product recommendations — HIPAA/SOC 2 and PCI/CMMC products highlighted in amber on the product list with reason text
Compliance badges on each product: ⚠ HIPAA/SOC2 or ⚠ PCI/CMMC when unselected, ✓ Compliance when added
Recommendation panel above the summary strip shows all unselected compliance products with one-click Add
Multi-term pricing preview — 12/24/36 month comparison cards above the quote summary, clickable to switch term
TCV shown for all three terms at a glance — no need to manually toggle to see other options
Payment method surcharge panel in cost model — ACH free, check +$10 admin, CC +2% surcharge with live dollar estimate
Payment fees stored in Admin → Pricing Settings — fully configurable as rates change
improved: Contract term discounts now only apply to labor + discountable add-ons — MSRP products added after discount calculation
Risk/compliance multipliers scoped to discountable revenue only
fixed: Previously a contract discount applied to full MRR including MSRP products — costing unrecoverable margin on every deal with security tooling

summary: Fix market tier / AI analysis disconnect — AI multiplier now drives Managed IT pricing

new: AI Market Recommendation banner appears in the Market Tier section when analysis is accepted — shows city, market tier, and exact pricing multiplier
Apply AI Rate button applies the AI-derived pricing multiplier directly to the quote calculation
Keep Manual option dismisses the recommendation and keeps the manually selected tier
Active AI rate shown as a green indicator above the tier list with one-click removal
Clicking any manual tier removes the AI override — clear visual feedback on which is active
AI multiplier saved with the quote and restored on reload
fixed: Market Rate Analysis card and Market Tier selector were completely disconnected — AI returned 'standard/adjusted/secondary/premium' but DB tiers use 'major_metro/mid_market/small_market' — now bypassed entirely with a precise pricing_multiplier
Static -10% / -20% tier labels no longer conflict with AI recommendations — both systems coexist cleanly
improved: calcQuote now accepts aiMultiplierOverride — takes precedence over DB tier labor_multiplier when AI analysis is active

summary: Bundle / Unbundle — convert any IT or Voice quote to a Bundle and back

new: Bundle with Voice button on saved Managed IT quotes — carries all client details, devices, users, products, HubSpot link, and market data into a new Bundle quote with IT pre-populated
Bundle with IT button on saved Hosted Voice quotes — carries all voice configuration, seat counts, hardware, and client details into a new Bundle quote with Voice pre-populated
Unbundle button on all Bundle quotes — opens a modal to choose Keep IT Only or Keep Hosted Voice Only
Keep IT Only — creates a new standalone IT quote with all device/user/product settings from the bundle intact
Keep Hosted Voice Only — creates a new standalone Voice quote with all seat/license/hardware settings from the bundle intact
Bundle quote remains in history after unbundling — nothing is deleted
All conversions preserve: client name, zip, market tier, HubSpot deal link, proposal details, and notes
improved: Routes /quotes/new, /voice/new, and /bundle/new added — enables navigation with pre-loaded state without clashing with existing quote IDs

summary: Onboarding Incentive Engine — AI-recommended split payments and discounts on every Managed IT quote

new: Onboarding Incentive card appears on every Managed IT quote below the one-time fee — AI recommends split payment or discount based on market tier and fee size
Split Payment option: $500–750 up to 3 months, $750–1500 up to 6 months, $1500+ up to 12 months — monthly installment added to MRR for the split period
Discount option: 10%, 20%, 30%, 50%, or Full Waive — reduces effective one-time fee
AI recommendation shown with reasoning — e.g. 'Secondary market — 50% discount can be the difference between winning and losing'
Accept or override the recommendation — both modes fully configurable
Legal default recovery note built in — all incentives forfeited on contract breach, full fee recoverable per MSA
Effective onboarding fee flows through to Contract Value, TCV, and quote save
Incentive details saved with the quote — mode, effective fee, monthly adder, discount pct, full original fee
formatIncentiveForExport() prepares incentive for Smart Pricing Table injection
improved: Contract Value and TCV now reflect effective onboarding fee when an incentive is applied
Onboarding summary card shows effective fee, not full fee, when incentive is active

summary: Admin → Market Rates tab + fix AI analysis engine

new: Admin → Market Rates tab — full index of all analyzed markets with search, tier filter, and expand-to-edit
Analyze New Market — type any zip, city, or state and run a fresh AI analysis on demand, no quote required
Expand any market row to view all rates, edit individual rates, and save manual overrides
Re-run AI Analysis button per market — refreshes with latest market intelligence
Stale indicators on markets older than 6 months — badge in table and count in header
improved: AI analysis engine now always returns city and state — cleaner DB matching and zip indexing
Fixed model name in AI analysis function — was using an invalid model string causing all analyses to fail

New market feedback — loading message now says 'Checking database — if this is a new market, we'll run an AI analysis. Stand by...' and a blue banner confirms when a new market has been analyzed and saved
fixed: Market Rate Analysis card now correctly triggers from zip code alone — was silently failing because lookupZip does not return city/state fields

summary: Market Rate Intelligence Engine — AI-powered, per-market labor rate analysis on every quote

new: Market Rate Analysis card on all three quote types — triggers automatically when zip code is applied
AI-powered market analysis for any city/state using CoL index, median income, local labor rates, and MSP market intelligence
61 markets pre-loaded from Ferrum market research (Apr 2026) — AI handles any market not in the seed data
Rates analyzed: Remote Support, On-Site Dispatch (2hr block), On-Site Additional Hourly, Dev/CRM/Web, Design/UX, PC Setup
After-hours rates auto-calculated at 1.5× the market on-site rate — dispatch fees scale with market CoL
FAIR / SLIGHTLY HIGH / HIGH / LOW rating for each rate vs. FerrumIT published rates
Accept All Rates button saves the market rate sheet to the quote — or override individual lines before accepting
Managed IT pricing multiplier recommendation shown — apply via the Market Tier selector to align package rates with market
Market intelligence notes per market — primary industries, MSP selling angles, compliance hooks
Last analyzed date always visible — auto-refreshes if analysis is older than 6 months
Manual Refresh button available on every market card
Analysis source shown: imported (from Apr 2026 research) or AI-generated (new markets)
improved: Static Area 2 surcharge (+30%) removed — replaced by real market rate analysis per city
Market tier selection now guided by AI recommendation — shows suggested tier and pricing multiplier

summary: HubSpot deal connect UX improvements and Deal Summary label fix

improved: Deal connect status messages now show in context-appropriate colors — blue for loading/fetching, amber for warnings, green for success, red for errors (was always red)
Deal Summary panel now shows 'Business Name' instead of 'Client' — more accurate before a deal is won

summary: Fix HubSpot deal URL and handle deals with no company association

fixed: HubSpot deal View link now uses correct URL format — old format was returning a HubSpot 404
Deals with no company associated in HubSpot now extract the business name from the deal name automatically
Warning message shown when no company is linked — prompts user to add address manually or link a company in HubSpot
Fix applies across all three quote types: Managed IT, Voice, and Bundle

summary: Fix HubSpot deal pull-in — business name, address, and zip now populate correctly

fixed: Recipient Business Name, Business Address, and Client Zip now populate when connecting a HubSpot deal — fixed by handling both v3 and v4 HubSpot association API response formats
If company name is blank in HubSpot, deal name is used as fallback for Recipient Business Name
Voice Quote: connecting a HubSpot deal now auto-applies the market tier from the client zip code (was setting zip but not updating tier)

summary: Fix hooks order crash in Send for Review review banner

fixed: ReviewBanner: moved useState and useEffect above conditional early return — prevented runtime crash when opening an in-review quote

summary: Send for Review workflow — route quotes for approval with email notification and HubSpot sync

new: Send for Review button on all quote types — appears after first save
Pick any team member as reviewer from a dropdown — sends them a direct email link
Optional note to reviewer: explain the discount, flag the competitive situation, etc.
Review banner shown to the reviewer at the top of the quote — yellow, hard to miss
Reviewer can Approve (turns quote green, status = Approved) or Return with Comments (back to Draft)
Feedback required before returning — reviewer must leave a comment
Both request and response automatically post as Quote Notes and to HubSpot activity timeline
Rep gets an email back with the decision and full feedback text
New quote statuses: In Review (amber) and Approved (green) — visible in Saved Quotes list
SPT export gate: Approved status is the future hook for Smart Pricing Table integration (noted in code)
improved: Saved Quotes status filter now includes In Review and Approved options
Status badges updated — In Review shows amber, Approved shows green

summary: Quote revision history — automatic field-level diff tracking on every save

new: Revision History panel on every quote — below Quote Notes, above nothing
Every save automatically creates a version snapshot — no manual steps required
Field-level diff view: see exactly what changed between saves (Users: 10 → 11, Package: Essentials → Plus, etc.)
Changes grouped by category: Quote, Details, Managed IT, Products, Voice, Pricing
Product adds and removes tracked explicitly — Added / Removed with product name
MRR delta shown per revision — +50/mo in red, -5/mo in green at a glance
Auto-generated one-line summary per revision: "Package, Users, Contract Term"
v1 always saved as Initial Save — clean starting point for every quote
Skips saving if nothing changed — no noise from accidental re-saves
Works across all three quote types: Managed IT, Voice, and Bundle
improved: What's New changelog added to portal — full release history from v1.0.0
Unread badge on What's New nav link shows how many releases since last visit

summary: Bundle quotes with tiered voice discount and free phone logic

new: Bundle Quote type — combines Managed IT + Voice into a single quote with one quote number
Bundle quotes get FIT-B- prefix for instant identification in the Saved Quotes list
Tiered voice bundle discount: 12-month IT = 5% off voice, 24-month = 10%, 36-month = 15%
Free phone qualification: 36-month contract + IT base MRR ≥ $750/mo (base package rates only — no add-ons)
Bundle savings banner shows monthly savings and total savings over contract term
Near-miss indicator: shows how much more IT MRR is needed to qualify for free phones
Collapsible IT and Voice sections in bundle form — each shows live MRR in header badge
Combined cost model and gross margin across both services in one view
improved: New Quote dropdown now has three options: Managed IT, Hosted Voice, Bundle Quote
Saved Quotes list shows gradient Bundle badge distinct from Voice badge
Bundle quotes route correctly from the Saved Quotes list

summary: Deal Description syncs to HubSpot on save; Quote Notes log posts to HubSpot activity timeline

new: Deal Description / Information field replaces Internal Notes — syncs to HubSpot deal description property on every save
Quote Notes log: append-only timestamped activity feed per quote — who added it, when, and what they said
Notes post to HubSpot as engagement notes, appearing in the deal's activity timeline with quote number and client name prefix
Notes are visible to all team members — full audit trail, no editing or deleting
Ctrl+Enter keyboard shortcut to submit notes quickly
HubSpot badge on individual notes confirms they were successfully posted
Save confirmation shows '· HubSpot updated' when description sync succeeds
improved: Both Managed IT and Voice quote pages use the same QuoteNotes component
Notes input disabled until quote is saved — prompts to save first
fixed: HubSpot note posting uses engagement notes API — appears in deal timeline, not just deal properties

summary: Hosted Voice module: per-seat, hybrid hosting, and SIP trunking quotes with crossover analysis

new: Voice Quote module with three quote types: Hosted Seats (per user), Hybrid Hosting (BYOPBX), SIP Trunking Only
Hosted Seats: Pro and Enterprise license tiers (3CX Pro/Enterprise), billable seat pricing, non-billable devices at $0 for full transparency
Hybrid Hosting: 3CX license tiers (Pro/Enterprise × 8/16/24 paths), AWS hosting, optional enhanced VM instance, Managed IT clients billed hosting-only
SIP Trunking: flat $19.95/concurrent call path
Crossover analysis: live comparison showing when hybrid hosting is cheaper than per-seat pricing
Numbers & DIDs: local DIDs ($1.50 w/CNAM), SMS-enabled DIDs (+$1.25), toll-free ($5.00), E911 ($2.50 flat), number porting ($25 NRC)
International dialing policy: none / standard / full open — waiver language auto-displays for elevated access
Virtual Fax packages: Email-Only ($9.95), Solo ($12), Team ($29), Business ($59), Infinity ($119), ATA device ($15 + $150 NRC)
Call recording, SMS/MMS with 10DLC registration and campaign fees, metered per-segment rates displayed for client acknowledgment
Yealink hardware: T33G/T43U/T46U/T48U — evergreen lease or outright purchase
Hardware discount logic: 36-month = free, 24-month = 50% off option
Estimated taxes card (20-30% configurable) with disclaimer language
Bundle discount: Managed IT clients toggle applies configurable % off voice MRR
All 18 voice pricing settings stored in Supabase — editable in Admin → Pricing Settings
HubSpot Connect Deal modal — auto-populates client details from deal company and contact
Voice quotes saved with purple Voice badge in Saved Quotes list
improved: New Quote button in nav becomes a dropdown with Managed IT and Hosted Voice options
Saved Quotes list routes Voice quotes to the Voice page, IT quotes to the IT page

summary: Separate sell and cost qty drivers per product; mobile devices field added

new: cost_qty_driver field on products — sell price and cost price can now use different quantity bases
SaaSAlerts: sells per user, costs per user (shared mailboxes no longer inflate cost)
INKY: sells and costs per mailbox (users + shared) — bills correctly for shared mailboxes
Mobile Devices field added to People & Devices section — phones and tablets
mobile_device qty driver added — products can now be priced per mobile device
Admin → Products shows both Qty Driver (Sell) and Qty Driver (Cost) fields
Quote line items show cost basis note when sell and cost drivers differ
improved: Pricing engine splits getSellQty and getCostQty for accurate margin tracking
Shared mailboxes field now correctly impacts only INKY pricing, not SaaSAlerts or DropSuite

summary: Market tier now adjusts sell price (Option B) for consistent margins nationwide

new: Market tier multiplier now applies to sell rates (WS, user, server, location, tenant, vendor) — not internal cost
Clients in smaller markets pay lower prices; FerrumIT maintains consistent margins everywhere
Floor protection ($350/mo minimum) still applies regardless of market adjustment
improved: Market tier label updated from '-10% labor' to '-10% pricing' throughout the app
Admin → Market Tiers field label updated to 'Pricing Multiplier' with clear description
Labor line in cost model no longer shows market multiplier (it no longer applies to cost)

summary: Flex time per package, package creation, location rate column, and product save fixes

new: Flex time per package: configurable minutes per workstation per month (0 = not included, positive = minutes, -1 = unlimited)
Flex time visual card on quote output: shows total hours/month with progress bar, or 'By Request' / 'Unlimited' banners
Non-rollover indicator on flex time card
Add Package button in Admin → Packages — create new tiers from scratch
Activate/Deactivate toggle on packages — disable without deleting
improved: Admin → Packages table now shows location rate column
Package modal title correctly shows 'New Package' vs 'Edit Package'
fixed: Product save was silently failing — now shows error messages in the modal
Product edit modal clears previous errors when opening a new record
Reload Config button now shows '✓ Reloaded' confirmation feedback
Products admin calls global config reload after saving so quote builder reflects changes immediately

summary: HubSpot full integration: Connect Deal auto-populates client fields, stage labels resolved, open deals only

new: Connect Deal modal on quote form — search open HubSpot deals by name
Selecting a deal auto-populates: client name, contact name, contact email, business address, zip code
Zip code auto-extracts from company address and sets market tier automatically
Stage names now show human-readable labels (e.g. 'Appointment Scheduled') not numeric IDs
Closed/Lost and Closed/Won deals filtered from search results using stage label matching
Netlify Function proxy routes all HubSpot API calls server-side — CORS resolved
Admin → Integrations tab: paste HubSpot token, Test Connection button
Deal sync button pushes updated MRR and contract value back to HubSpot deal
Proposal Name, Recipient Business Name, Contact Name, Contact Email, Business Address fields added to quote form
improved: HubSpot token stored securely in Supabase — never in code or environment variables
Test Connection routes through proxy (fixed network error on direct browser call)
fixed: Zip code 60124 (Elgin, IL) now correctly shows Chicago suburbs instead of Hartford, CT
Full ZIP name lookup table expanded — 300+ metro areas with correct 3-digit prefix mapping

summary: Microsoft SSO login, admin panel, activity log, and quote notes foundation

new: Microsoft M365 SSO login — single click, no passwords, uses existing FerrumIT accounts
Auto-creates Supabase profile on first login from Microsoft identity
Admin panel with tabs: Products, Packages, Market Tiers, Pricing Settings, Users, Integrations
Full CRUD on products: name, category, sell price, cost price, qty driver, exclusive groups
Gross margin preview in product edit modal
Activate/Deactivate toggle on products — historical quotes not affected
Market Tiers admin: add/edit tiers, set pricing multipliers
Pricing Settings admin: edit any rate (onboarding, stack costs, burdened rate, contract discounts) without code changes
Users admin: set roles (admin/user), view last login
Activity Log: every change tracked with who, what field changed, old and new values, expandable diff view
Saved Quotes list: search by client name, filter by status, click to open any quote
Quote numbers auto-generated as FIT-YYYY-NNNN on save
improved: Login page shows 'Sign in with Microsoft' button with Microsoft logo
Nav shows Admin and Activity Log links for admin users only
User avatar with initials shown in top nav
fixed: Supabase invite links pointed to localhost — fixed by updating Site URL in Supabase Auth settings

summary: Initial release: Managed IT quote builder with DB-driven pricing, market tiers, and add-on products

new: Managed IT quote builder with live pricing engine
Package selection: Business Essentials, Business Plus, Enterprise
People & Devices inputs: users, shared mailboxes, workstations, endpoints, servers, locations, cloud tenants, vendors
Market tier selection with zip code lookup — auto-detects metro area and suggests tier
Risk & Compliance multipliers: industry risk, compliance level, complexity, coverage hours
Add-on product catalog: Cloud & Email Security, Endpoint Security, Backup & Recovery, Security Awareness, SIEM & SOC, Network & Connectivity, Strategic Advisory
Exclusive group logic — selecting one INKY tier deselects others automatically
Contract term discounts: 12mo (5%), 24mo (10%), 36mo (20%)
Executive reporting flag triggers Enterprise package recommendation
Cost model: tooling cost + service hours × burdened rate = total cost with implied GM
Onboarding fee calculator with $500 floor
Smart Pricing Table JSON export on every saved quote
All pricing data stored in Supabase — zero hardcoded rates
Supabase Auth with email/password, RLS policies on all tables
Deployed to Netlify with GitHub auto-deploy
